programmes.show_description: British character actor Alun Armstrong stars in the hit cop series featuring a veteran detective team. A desperate Halford visits a clairvoyant hoping to talk to his wife.
The thorny subject of the contraceptive pill causes controversy, with Dr Turner's support putting him at odds with some of his more traditional colleagues.
Scheissfeld: Miep and Jan's marriage is tested when he ignores her plea to abandon a dangerous mission. After the Nazis suffer a huge blow at the hands of The Resistance, Jan hides.
Aileen Aroon: More corpses of young men are found, while other teens continue to disappear, and Corcoran leaves no stone unturned in his search for answers.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
You Pays Yer Money: Sharon isn't registered to vote in Chigwell, but when election fever hits the constituency, she wants to make use of Darryl's vote.
Love and War: Part 2: Quinnan's secret comes out, dividing Sun Hill. Crime fighting takes a back seat as Quinnan and Garfield clash.
New. Hopelessly Devoted: Michelle helps a patient with rheumatoid arthritis who suspects her partner may be cheating on her and Joe confronts Daniel over his suspicious behaviour.
New. Out With the Bath Water: Melody forces a man to face the issues he has with fatherhood and Lionel poses an important question to Vivien.
Protection: Ed's father collapses while working in the hospital canteen and Ric moves out of Jess and Lisa's flat. Mubbs struggles to adjust to Rosie and her children living with him.
Making Other Plans: Adam finally forgives himself for Harry's death following an encounter with psychopathic prisoner Jackson.
Love and War: Part 2: Quinnan's secret comes out, dividing Sun Hill. Crime fighting takes a back seat as Quinnan and Garfield clash.
New. As Pat fights for her life in hospital, fingers begin to point at Janine - will she come clean? Max has exciting news for Bradley, and Ronnie lets Roxy in on her secret.
New. As Roxy plans her father's funeral, she discovers shocking news; will she ever be able to forgive Peggy? Janine has trouble at the police station, and Syed returns.
Big Steps and Little Uns: A voyage back in time to a more innocent age to meet the Yorkshire vets. Siegfried, James and Tristan deal with domestic disharmony in their own ways.
Amanda's father confronts Leo, Charles waits anxiously for Avril to recover, and Ken basks smugly in the glory of having avoided suspicion for the theft of Jan's designs.
No More Cakes and Ale: In a race against the clock, Frank and Lu must track down a vital witness in a crown court trial.
Poetic Justice: Fletcher is incensed when a third cellmate arrives - and turns out to be an old enemy. But why is everyone so polite to him? Includes strong language.
Who's That with Barry and Glenda? - It's Not Barry and Glenda: Classic comedy. Truly reckons Barry is up to something sinister - why else would he be in the woods with a suitcase?
An Apple a Day: The long running sitcom that first graced our screens in 1973. Clegg, Truly and Alvin reluctantly agree to go to a remote farm and take some old-timers out for a day.
Barry Becomes a Psychopathic Killer but Only Part Time: Roy Clarke's classic sitcom. Truly helps Smiler when he's pursued by a woman who thinks he's rich.
Hard-Gating: Martin Shaw is the tough but very fair High Court Judge tackling another mystery. A prison inmate is found dead in his cell, seemingly killed by his cell mate.
British character actor Alun Armstrong stars in the hit cop series featuring a veteran detective team. A desperate Halford visits a clairvoyant hoping to talk to his wife.
No More Cakes and Ale: In a race against the clock, Frank and Lu must track down a vital witness in a crown court trial.
Mens Sana: Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star in the top-rated crime drama series. Pascoe investigates when a woman is found dead at a private health club. Dalziel books in!
You Pays Yer Money: Sharon isn't registered to vote in Chigwell, but when election fever hits the constituency, she wants to make use of Darryl's vote.
The Children of the Battlefield: Corcoran closes in on those responsible for the kidnapping and murder of young Five Points men.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Trust: Tracey falls for her smooth therapist Gerald and argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship when he bans her from continuing her sessions.
Hot Money: Burnside and Meadows take a gamble when they set up an undercover operation in a local casino.
New. Foreign Affairs: Daniel and Joanna go on their first real date, Archie helps a macho patient deal with a dubious rash and Ronnie tells George he is filing for divorce.
New. Another Day: A difficult cancer patient falls out with Melody. Archie and Michelle play tricks on Vivien, but she goes along with the game and turns the tables on them.
Honour Thy Father: Ric and Diane are thrown together by the death of a patient, while Ed goes behind Tom's back to get a second opinion when he is told his father's heart is beyond repair.
The Enemy Within: Zoe realises she cannot keep up the pretence of her pregnancy much longer. Lenny faces a tough decision regarding the Jafa fellowship.
Hot Money: Burnside and Meadows take a gamble when they set up an undercover operation in a local casino.
New. It is Archie's funeral, and Marsden is watching the suspects like a hawk. Will the murderer slip up? A surprise guest turns up at the wake.
New. Glenda is summoned to the Vic as the Mitchells grill the surprise arrival. Meanwhile, Bradley plucks up the courage to propose to Stacey, but she has other worries on her mind.
One of Nature's Little Miracles: Fun with the vets making their mark on Yorkshire. Siegfried takes on another assistant who bizarrely brings his badger with him.
Charles learns that Avril has discharged herself from hospital. Jan considers the future of her fashion business, while Jack and Tom remain at loggerheads.
The Offered Fallacy: Frank and Lu must clear their names when they are framed for a string of crimes by some mysterious doppelgangers.
Rough Justice: Fletcher decides that Harris must be put on trial for stealing from another prisoner. You can't trust anyone.
Things to Do When Your Wife Runs off with a Turkish Waiter: More pensioner power in the much-loved sitcom. Barry and Glenda are surprised when a man they met on holiday pays a visit.
Beware of Laughing at Nora's Hats: Another slice of senior silliness. Nora reveals an ongoing feud with one Audrey Craig, who had the audacity to laugh about her hat at a wedding.
Yours Truly - If You're Not Careful: Young-at-heart sitcom. Billy and Alvin terrorise Truly with the news that the former Mrs Truelove is in town and trying to track him down.
Hidden Treasures: When Dolly complains about strange occurrences at home, Pearl is concerned about her mental well-being but sets up security cameras - leading to a surprising discovery.
To Those We Love: Pearl caters a wedding at a theme park, and when the bride found dead in the hall of mirrors she can't resist investigating - even when it leads her straight into danger.
The Sweeney meets The Likely Lads in the hit cop show. The squad investigate the death of a barrister found in his car 20 years earlier.
